summary: Windows exposes a privacy setting for whether websites can access the user's language list. Microsoft documents the exact HttpAcceptLanguageOptOut registry value under the user's International profile, and the current app writes that same control surface.

setting.name: Website Access to Language List
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can let websites use your language list to show locally relevant content. This setting controls whether that language-list access is allowed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off when they do not want websites learning or using their preferred language list for personalization.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off can reduce language-based personalization, but some sites may stop using the language list to tailor content automatically.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows privacy guidance documents both the UI behavior and the exact registry mapping: create REG_DWORD `HttpAcceptLanguageOptOut` under `HKCU\Control Panel\International\User Profile` and set it to `1` to turn the feature off. The app writes that exact documented surface.

allowed_values.label: Windows managed UI state
allowed_values.meaning: The setting stays under normal Windows UI control with no explicit registry opt-out override.

allowed_values.label: Website access to language list disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Websites are prevented from using the user's language list for locally relevant content.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes HttpAcceptLanguageOptOut = 1 under the user's International profile. Microsoft documents that exact registry mapping for turning the setting off.

windows_defaults.label: Windows UI managed default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows user profiles using the normal Privacy settings UI

states.rationale: Leave the documented opt-out value absent and let Windows manage the setting through the UI.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline keeps the privacy toggle under normal Windows management.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
avoid_for: Research-only publishing of the current registry write

states.rationale: Leave the opt-out registry value absent.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to manage the setting through the normal privacy UI.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You explicitly want websites blocked from using the language list.
tradeoffs: Language-based personalization remains under normal Windows control.
No explicit opt-out override is forced.

recommended_profiles.label: No language list access
intended_for: Users who do not want websites reading their language list
avoid_for: Users who rely on language-list-based website personalization

states.rationale: Use the Microsoft-documented registry opt-out value.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want websites blocked from using the language list for locally relevant content.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want sites to keep using that language-list hint.
tradeoffs: Some sites may stop tailoring content from the user's preferred language list.

evidence.title: Microsoft privacy guidance for language list access
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the UI behavior and directly says to create REG_DWORD HttpAcceptLanguageOptOut under HKCU\Control Panel\International\User Profile with value 1 to turn the feature off.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HttpAcceptLanguageOptOut = 1 under HKCU\Control Panel\International\User Profile.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact `HttpAcceptLanguageOptOut` registry value under `HKCU\Control Panel\International\User Profile` and states that setting it to `1` turns off website access to the user's language list. The app writes that exact documented control surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Create a new REG_DWORD registry setting named HttpAcceptLanguageOptOut in HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Control Panel\International\User Profile with a value of 1.
validation_proof.notes: The same Microsoft privacy guidance page documents the related UI toggle and the exact registry mapping for turning the feature off.
